headline: Arkansas QB Jackson leads Razorbacks past Boston College in Week 1 offensive showcase
body: Arkansas made a statement in its season opener, overwhelming Boston College 29-20 with a balanced attack that exposed the Eagles' defensive vulnerabilities from snap one. K.J. Jackson orchestrated the Razorback offense with precision, completing 19 of 34 passes for 288 yards and two touchdowns while managing the game's tempo effectively. The Arkansas ground game added another layer of concern for Boston College, churning out 188 rushing yards as the Eagles struggled to establish any consistent rhythm on either side of the ball.

Boston College's offensive woes began with insufficient time in the pocket and a depleted rushing attack that netted only 95 yards on the afternoon. Quarterback Leavitt worked hard within a compressed pocket, hitting 13 of 23 attempts for 180 yards and one score, but the Eagles' inability to sustain drives killed their momentum. Receiver Skeete provided bright moments with 53 yards on three receptions, yet it proved insufficient against an Arkansas secondary that rarely allowed Boston College to establish anything resembling offensive rhythm. The turnover margin remained even, but field position tilted heavily toward the Razorbacks throughout.

Arkansas' pass rush made the difference in critical moments, with linebacker Stafford recording two sacks that forced Boston College into third-and-long situations repeatedly. Parker's 91 receiving yards highlighted how completely Arkansas controlled the perimeter game, creating separation that Boston College's secondary simply could not match. By the time the fourth quarter arrived, the Eagles faced a double-digit deficit they could not overcome, and Arkansas' depth on both lines dictated the tempo from start to finish.
turningPoint: Arkansas established a 2-0 sack advantage midway through the second quarter, forcing Boston College into desperation mode and allowing the Razorbacks to lean on Washington's rushing attack without defensive pressure.

unsungHero.name: Stafford
unsungHero.why: While Jackson earned Player of the Game honors for his passing numbers, Stafford's two sacks set the tone for Arkansas' defensive dominance and directly forced Boston College into predictable third-down situations that dictated the game's flow
